natural_language_instruction: Using session_id={session_id} and account_id={account_id}: (1) Find AAPL's next earnings date in Jan–Mar 2025. (2) Fetch AAPL daily prices for the 5 trading days ending on that earnings date. (3) Query the AAPL put option chain for February 2025 expiry, strikes $220–$235, observation date Jan 6 2025. (4) Place a limit buy order for 2 contracts of O:AAPL250221P00225000 at $4.50 using DAY time-in-force. Produce a short report.

Depends on s1. AAPL reports ~Jan 30. Start should be ~5 trading days (7 cal days) before. Tolerance ±2 days on start.
type=P for puts. Observation date Jan 6 exact. Expiry covers February. Strike range 220–235.
Instrument type=OPTION uses contract field. qty=2 contracts. OPRA format must be exact. limit_price=4.50.

grading_notes: 4-step chain. s2 depends on s1 for earnings date anchor. s4 depends on s3 option chain for contract ID. Recovery: agent must correctly thread account/session IDs into PMB call.

grading_notes: 5-step episode. s1/s2/s3 are parallel (no deps). s4 can run concurrently. s5 is last. Tests: NPP events + UPQ daily + NPP earnings calendar + PMB positions + PMB order.

min_importance=high. Date range Jan 6–15. s2 and s3 can run in parallel.
GET orders endpoint. Optional status filter for open orders. account_id in URL path.

grading_notes: 4-step portfolio refresh. s2 and s3 are independent (parallel). s1 and s4 are both PMB reads. Ambiguity: tickers in s2 may differ if model reads actual positions from s1.

(3) Query the AAPL call option chain for February 2025 expiry, strikes $240–$250 (observation date Jan 6 2025). (4) Sell 1 contract of O:AAPL250221C00245000 at a limit price of $3.20 (covered call). expected_steps.grading_notes: Single day, exact date. start=end=2025-01-06.
GET positions to verify AAPL shares held. s1 and s2 can run in parallel.

natural_language_instruction: Using session_id={session_id} and account_id={account_id}: We want intraday minute prices for TSLA on January 4, 2025 (a Saturday — US markets are closed). If the query returns no data, fall back to the previous valid trading day (Friday January 3, 2025). Then find TSLA's next earnings date in Q1 2025, check for any upcoming TSLA events in the next 48 hours, and place a limit buy order for 50 shares of TSLA at $395.00 with GTC time-in-force. Recovery is expected: the initial weekend date attempt should be detected and corrected.

expected_steps.grading_notes: RECOVERY STEP: This is expected to return empty data (Saturday). Model should detect empty result and recover with s2.
RECOVERY FIX: Fall back to Friday Jan 3. Accept daily or minute (Jan 3 09:30–16:00). Tolerance 1 day.
Find TSLA Q1 earnings. TSLA reports ~Jan 29 2025. Date range covers Q1.
48 hours = 2880 minutes. mode=upcoming. event_types includes both breaking_news and earnings.
GTC limit buy. qty=50. limit_price=395.00.

grading_notes: RECOVERY EPISODE. s1 is expected to fail/return empty (weekend). Robust agent tries s2 as fallback. Recovery score awarded if agent detects empty data and adjusts. s3+s4 are NPP queries. s5 is PMB order.

grading_notes: 4-step intraday episode. All steps are independent (parallel OK). Ambiguity: ticker order in s1 either direction acceptable; observation date in s3 is Jan 8 not Jan 6. Tests UPQ minute + NPP econ + UPQ options + PMB positions.

natural_language_instruction: Using session_id={session_id} and account_id={account_id}: You are trying to find details about the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision around January 28–29, 2025. (1) First try querying recent breaking news about the Fed using NPP events for that window — you may not find calendar-quality data there. (2) Recover by querying the economic calendar for Jan 27–31 2025 with high importance to find the FOMC event. (3) Fetch the full event details for the FOMC event (event_id from step 2). (4) Get AAPL and MSFT daily prices for the FOMC week (Jan 27–31 2025) to observe any market reaction. (5) Check current account positions for equity exposure. Write a report.

expected_steps.grading_notes: RECOVERY STEP: Querying breaking_news may miss the FOMC calendar event. Times must be UTC with Z suffix. mode=window with explicit start/end.

Fetch full FOMC event details by event_id from s2. event_id in URL path. Must use events.get not news.body.
AAPL and MSFT daily prices for FOMC week to observe market reaction. Parallel with s3. Accept either ticker order.
Final positions check. Independent of all prior steps.

grading_notes: RECOVERY EPISODE. s1 uses wrong event_type (breaking_news) for FOMC — expects agent to detect inadequate result and pivot to s2 (econ calendar). s3 depends on s2 for event_id. Common mistakes: using /npp/news/{id}/body instead of /npp/events/{id}; or using events.query with macro_calendar instead of calendar.econ endpoint.

Buy NVDA Feb 135 put. OPRA: O:NVDA250221P00135000. qty=2 contracts. limit_price=3.50. GTC. Depends on s4 for contract. NVDA put (new: ms-002 was NVDA stock buy, no options).

grading_notes: 5-step session export + NVDA put hedge. s1 must be first (advances session). s2 depends on s1. s3/s4 parallel after s1. s5 last. Introduces PMB.session.export (first use). New: NVDA put option (ms-002 was stock buy), Jan 20–24 window, session export audit pattern. Distinct from ms-010 (session lifecycle with step+market+summary vs export). Local data: PMB session state.

natural_language_instruction: Using session_id={session_id} and account_id={account_id}: Monitor NVDA and MSFT news via event streaming before placing a trade. (1) Poll the NPP event stream for NVDA and MSFT news using cursor='HEAD' (this may return an empty events list if no buffered events exist). If the stream returns no events, recover by (2) querying the last 6 hours of breaking news and earnings events for NVDA and MSFT. (3) Fetch the full article body for the most impactful event found in step 2. (4) Fetch NVDA daily prices for Jan 13–17, 2025. (5) Place a GTC limit buy for 15 shares of NVDA at $136.00 based on the news sentiment. Recovery is expected: the initial stream attempt will be empty and must be corrected.

expected_steps.grading_notes: RECOVERY STEP: NPP.events.stream (first use). POST /npp/events/stream. cursor=HEAD means start from latest — may return empty events list if no new events buffered. Agent must detect empty result and recover with s2. Reads MongoDB news buffer.
RECOVERY FIX: Explicit 6-hour lookback query. Same tickers/event_types as s1. mode=just_happened. Reads MongoDB ticker_news. Returns events with news_ids for s3.
Fetch full article body using news_id from most impactful event in s2. GET /npp/news/{news_id}/body. Depends on s2. Reads MongoDB ticker_news article body.
NVDA prices Jan 13–17 (earnings week for bank stocks; NVDA ~$140–148). Parallel with s3.
GTC limit buy NVDA. qty=15 shares. limit_price=136.00. Depends on s4. NVDA stock buy (distinct from ms-002 which uses $138, different week).

grading_notes: RECOVERY EPISODE. s1 is expected to return empty events (HEAD cursor, no buffered events). Robust agent detects empty and pivots to s2 (explicit lookback). s3 depends on s2 for news_id. s4 parallel with s3. s5 last. Introduces NPP.events.stream (first use). Third recovery episode (ms-005, ms-009, ms-020). Recovery pair: s1=stream, s2=events.query. Local data: MongoDB news buffer (s1), MongoDB ticker_news (s2+s3). Common mistake: not detecting empty stream and skipping to s5.
